name: German Pumpernickel Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, stir together yeast, cocoa, sugar, caraway seed, salt, and 2 cups rye flour; set aside.
2. In a 2-quart saucepan over low heat, heat water, molasses, and butter until very warm (120-130°F).
3. Using a mixer on low speed, gradually beat the molasses mixture into the yeast mixture until well blended. Increase to medium and beat for 2 minutes.
4. Add the remaining 1 cup rye flour. Increase to high and beat for an additional 2 minutes.
5. Stir in enough all-purpose flour to make a soft dough. Turn out onto a lightly floured surface. Knead until smooth and elastic, about 5 minutes.
6. Place into a greased large bowl, turning the dough so that the top is greased. Cover with a towel and let rise in a warm place until almost doubled, about 45 minutes to an hour.
7. Punch down dough. Divide in half. Cover and let rest for 5 minutes.
8. Shape each half into a round loaf. Place, 4 inches apart, on a greased large baking sheet.
9. Cover and let rise until almost doubled, 45 minutes to an hour.
10. Diagonally slash each loaf, crosswise, 3 times.
11. Bake in a 375°F oven for 20 minutes. Cover loosely with foil; bake another 15 minutes or until loaves sound hollow when tapped.
12. Immediately remove from baking sheet. Brush tops of hot loaves with shortening. Cool on racks. Makes 2 loaves.
